Michigan Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity & How to Apply

Already licensed elsewhere? Start with compact status, endorsement requirements, fees, and your likely transfer timeline.

Last verified 2026-02-25
No Interstate Compact

How to Transfer Your Speech-Language Pathologist License to Michigan

There is no active interstate compact covering speech-language pathologist practice in Michigan. To work in Michigan, apply for licensure by endorsement through Board of Speech-Language Pathology.

Endorsement fee
$118
Processing time
4 to 6 weeks
Fingerprinting
Required
Temporary license
Available
See endorsement steps Official board portal

Endorsement Application Steps

  1. Earn a Master's Degree in SLP

    Complete a master's degree from a CAA-accredited program.

  2. Pass the Praxis Examination

    Achieve a passing score on Praxis (test code 5331, score 162).

  3. Complete Clinical Fellowship

    Complete 36 weeks of supervised clinical practice (1,260 hours).

  4. Submit Application to LARA

    Submit application with fees through LARA online system.

Quick Facts

Total fee
$118
Processing time
4 to 6 weeks
Renewal fee
$118
Renewal cycle
Every 2 years

Required Documents

  • Official graduate transcripts: From a CAA-accredited program.
  • Praxis exam score report: Score of 162 or higher.
  • Clinical Fellowship verification: CF completion documentation.
  • Background check: Criminal background check.

Board Contact

Board
Board of Speech-Language Pathology
Department
Michigan Department of Licensing and Regulatory Affairs (LARA)
Phone
(517) 335-0918
Email
bplhelp@michigan.gov

Visit official board portal

Renewal

Renewal fee is $118. Renewal cycle: Every 2 years.

Michigan Speech-Language Pathologist Reciprocity FAQ

How much does a Michigan SLP license cost?

$118 initial. $118 biennial renewal.

Is Michigan part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

No. Michigan has not enacted ASLP-IC legislation as of 2026.

Does Michigan allow telepractice for SLPs?

Yes, with a valid Michigan license.

Does Michigan have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in Michigan. To work in Michigan, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Michigan?

Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Can I start working in Michigan while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
